# Wire provider routing and native Ollama runtime paths

## Context

Sub-2 of #10103, graduated from Discussion #11961. This sub owns runtime provider routing and native Ollama wire-up.

## The Problem

The config surface advertises provider choices such as `ollama` and `openAiCompatible`, but active chat, embedding, and graph-processing callsites are not consistently routed through a split provider contract. That makes cloud deployment configuration appear supported while runtime behavior may still fall back to OpenAI-compatible or Gemini-specific paths.

## The Architectural Reality

Primary surfaces:

- `ai/provider/Ollama.mjs`
- `ai/provider/OpenAiCompatible.mjs`
- `ai/services/memory-core/TextEmbeddingService.mjs`
- `ai/services/memory-core/SessionService.mjs`
- Dream / graph-mutator provider reachability gates

This is runtime-provider file-group scope. Tier-1 config key migration belongs to Sub-1; docs/integration readiness belongs to Sub-3.

## The Fix

Add or repair provider dispatch so `chatProvider` and `embeddingProvider` values route to native Ollama and OpenAI-compatible implementations correctly. Verify whether `Neo.ai.provider.Ollama` already supports embeddings; add a narrow embeddings method if not.

## Contract Ledger Matrix

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback / Edge Case | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Chat provider routing | #11961 OQ2/OQ6 | `chatProvider: 'ollama'` routes native Ollama chat where supported | OpenAI-compatible Ollama endpoint remains fallback profile | Provider JSDoc | Unit tests with mocked provider |
| Embedding provider routing | #11961 OQ2/OQ6 | `embeddingProvider: 'ollama'` routes native Ollama embeddings or adds missing method | If provider lacks embeddings, add explicit method rather than silently falling back | Provider JSDoc | Unit tests for embedding dispatch |
| Dream/graph-mutator readiness gate | #11961 OQ6 | Provider reachability checks align with selected chat provider | Manual/operator override path documented if needed | Service JSDoc | Focused dream readiness mock |

## Decision Record impact

Aligned with the #11961 decision that native Ollama is active, not deprecated.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Runtime callsites distinguish chat and embedding provider axes.
- [ ] Native Ollama chat routing is covered.
- [ ] Native Ollama embedding routing is covered, including adding the provider method if missing.
- [ ] OpenAI-compatible-with-Ollama remains supported as a common-subset fallback.
- [ ] Dream/REM graph-mutator provider reachability is not hardwired to the wrong provider family.
- [ ] Tests cover negative path: unsupported provider names fail explicitly instead of silently falling back to a different family.

## Out of Scope

- Tier-1 config key migration.
- Cloud documentation and full integration readiness tests.
- Per-tenant provider variation.

## Avoided Traps

- Do not treat native Ollama as speculative just because dispatch is incomplete.
- Do not collapse chat and embeddings back into one vague `modelProvider` axis.

## Related

- Parent: #10103
- Discussion: #11961
- Sub-1: config ownership ledger + Tier-1 lift
- Sub-3: cloud docs + readiness tests

Handoff Retrieval Hint: "Sub-2 #10103 runtime provider routing native Ollama chat embedding dream readiness"

- 2026-05-25T10:56:03Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `c968936` - "feat(memory-core): graph-provider dispatch + #11965 AC5 close (#11965 cycle-3)

Resolves @neo-gpt cycle-2 follow-up review CHANGES_REQUESTED on PR #11966 —
closes the graph-mutator provider-reachability blocker (#11965 AC5).

Per the #11965 Contract Ledger row "Dream/graph-mutator readiness gate" and
the operator client-need signal that flipped OQ2 disposition during
Discussion #11961 graduation (cloud-deployment clients prefer Ollama; no Mac
OS in cloud rules out MLX), graph-generation services must honor the active
`modelProvider` instead of hardwiring `OpenAiCompatible`.

Before: `SemanticGraphExtractor.mjs` (2 callsites), `GoldenPathSynthesizer.mjs`
(1 callsite), and `TopologyInferenceEngine.mjs` (1 callsite) all directly
instantiated `Neo.create(OpenAiCompatible, {...})`. Cloud deployments
configured for native Ollama would still see graph generation hit
OpenAI-compatible endpoints — speculative-support pattern.

After: shared `buildGraphProvider({modelProvider, ollamaConfig,
openAiCompatibleConfig, ...})` helper in `ai/services/graph/providerDispatch.mjs`
dispatches based on `aiConfig.modelProvider`. Pure function with injectable
provider factories. Throws explicitly for unsupported `modelProvider` (no
silent fallback). All 4 callsites migrated.

`SemanticGraphExtractor.consumerModel` (used for `invokeWithGuardrail`
telemetry) now reflects the active modelProvider so friction-aggregation logs
attribute work to the right provider family.

Gemini graph dispatch is intentionally out of Sub-2 scope (different API
shape; deferred to Sub-3 / follow-up). The default
`modelProvider: 'gemini'` in Memory Core's config is fine for chat
(`SessionService.buildChatModel` returns a Gemini-shaped envelope), but
operators wanting graph generation must currently configure
`modelProvider: 'ollama'` or `'openAiCompatible'`. Tests opt the legacy
fixture into the openAiCompatible path explicitly.

Test evidence: 25/25 PASS across the affected memory-core + graph specs.
7 new tests in `providerDispatch.spec.mjs` cover selector boundaries +
factory pass-through + envelope shape per provider."
- 2026-05-25T11:21:17Z @tobiu referenced in commit `58d352b` - "feat(memory-core): native Ollama runtime dispatch + provider.embed() method (#11965 Sub-2) (#11966)

* feat(ai/provider): add native Ollama embed() method (#11965 Sub-2 stage 1)

Sub-2 of #10103 (graduated from #11961). Stage 1: add embed() method to
Neo.ai.provider.Ollama using Ollama's native /api/embed endpoint.

V-B-A confirmed (#11961 OQ2 disposition): native Ollama is ACTIVE wire-up
target. Chat (generate/stream) was already implemented; embed was missing.

Method shape:
- Accepts single string OR array-of-strings for input (Ollama native API)
- Returns {embeddings: Number[][], raw: Object} — always 2D array for
  caller uniformity even with single-string input
- Uses configured embeddingModel; falls back to modelName chat model if
  embeddingModel unset (Ollama supports embeddings from many chat models
  but dedicated embedding models like nomic-embed-text are typical)
- Same native http(s).request shape as generate() (1h timeout, no fetch)

Config slot added:
- embeddingModel: null (dedicated embedding model, falls back to modelName)

Next stages:
- Stage 2: wire case 'ollama' in TextEmbeddingService runtime dispatch
- Stage 3: wire case 'ollama' in SessionService runtime dispatch
- Stage 4: observational branch cleanup (HealthService + GoldenPathSynthesizer)

Refs #11965 (Sub-2 native Ollama wire-up)
Refs #11961 (graduated Discussion)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>

* feat(memory-core): wire native Ollama dispatch in TextEmbeddingService (#11965 Sub-2 stage 2)

Stage 2 of #11965 (Sub-2 of #10103). Wires `explicitProvider === 'ollama'`
branches in TextEmbeddingService.embedText + embedTexts to dispatch to the
native Neo.ai.provider.Ollama instance (Stage 1 added its embed() method).

Implementation:
- New private `#getOllamaProvider()` lazy-init + cache on the singleton's
  reactive `ollamaProvider_` config slot. Reads host/model/embeddingModel
  from `aiConfig.ollama` block. Tests inject a fake via direct singleton
  property assignment to bypass lazy-init.
- embedText('text', 'ollama') → provider.embed(text); returns embeddings[0]
  (single-text variant projects from Ollama's always-2D response).
- embedTexts(['t1', 't2'], 'ollama') → provider.embed([...]); returns
  embeddings array directly (batch variant matches Ollama's array-of-arrays
  shape).

5 new tests:
1. embedText dispatches to Ollama when explicitProvider='ollama'
2. embedTexts dispatches batch to Ollama when explicitProvider='ollama'
3. embedText returns undefined when Ollama returns empty embeddings array
4. embedText with explicitProvider='openAiCompatible' does NOT dispatch to Ollama
5. embedText with explicitProvider='gemini' does NOT dispatch to Ollama

7/7 PASS (2 existing + 5 new).

Next stages:
- Stage 3: SessionService chat dispatch (case 'ollama')
- Stage 4: observational branch cleanup (HealthService + GoldenPathSynthesizer)

Refs #11965 (Sub-2 native Ollama wire-up)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>

* feat(memory-core): wire native Ollama chat dispatch in SessionService (#11965 Sub-2 stage 3)

Stage 3 of #11965. Wires modelProvider=ollama branch in SessionService.construct()
to instantiate Neo.ai.provider.Ollama and expose the Gemini-shaped
`this.model.generateContent` shim for downstream consumers
(summarizeSession, invokeWithGuardrail).

Implementation:
- New `else if (aiConfig.modelProvider === 'ollama')` branch in construct()
  between openAiCompatible and gemini paths
- Creates Neo.ai.provider.Ollama instance from aiConfig.ollama.{host, model,
  embeddingModel} with reasonable defaults (host: 127.0.0.1:11434, model: gemma4)
- Per-request config refresh matches openAiCompatible-path semantic: runtime
  config mutation by tests / embedded operators still applies to subsequent
  invocations
- Wraps provider.generate() in Gemini-compatible response envelope:
  `{response: {text: () => content}}`

Additional update:
- consumerModel naming logic at line 472 extended to include ollama branch
  (returns aiConfig.ollama.model for guardrail/log surfaces); pure ternary
  expansion, no semantic shift

Testing note:
- TextEmbeddingService dispatch (Stage 2) is unit-tested with injected
  provider seam (7/7 PASS)
- SessionService construct() dispatch is structurally symmetric to Stage 2
  but uses singleton-construct lifecycle that resists clean unit test injection
  in the existing spec env-setup. Full integration coverage of this path
  belongs to Sub-3 (#11964 cloud readiness tests for embeddings/chat/dream)
  per the ticket scope — Sub-2 owns the wiring; Sub-3 owns the end-to-end
  readiness proof.

Next stage:
- Stage 4: observational branch acknowledgment (HealthService +
  GoldenPathSynthesizer case 'ollama' branches transition from
  "vestigial half-support" to "active observational" — no code change
  required; existing branches were always correct, what was missing was
  the runtime dispatch this PR adds)

Refs #11965 (Sub-2 native Ollama wire-up)

closes the graph-mutator provider-reachability blocker (#11965 AC5).

Per the #11965 Contract Ledger row "Dream/graph-mutator readiness gate" and
the operator client-need signal that flipped OQ2 disposition during
Discussion #11961 graduation (cloud-deployment clients prefer Ollama; no Mac
OS in cloud rules out MLX), graph-generation services must honor the active
`modelProvider` instead of hardwiring `OpenAiCompatible`.

Before: `SemanticGraphExtractor.mjs` (2 callsites), `GoldenPathSynthesizer.mjs`
(1 callsite), and `TopologyInferenceEngine.mjs` (1 callsite) all directly
instantiated `Neo.create(OpenAiCompatible, {...})`. Cloud deployments
configured for native Ollama would still see graph generation hit
OpenAI-compatible endpoints — speculative-support pattern.

After: shared `buildGraphProvider({modelProvider, ollamaConfig,
openAiCompatibleConfig, ...})` helper in `ai/services/graph/providerDispatch.mjs`
dispatches based on `aiConfig.modelProvider`. Pure function with injectable
provider factories. Throws explicitly for unsupported `modelProvider` (no
silent fallback). All 4 callsites migrated.

`SemanticGraphExtractor.consumerModel` (used for `invokeWithGuardrail`
telemetry) now reflects the active modelProvider so friction-aggregation logs
attribute work to the right provider family.

Gemini graph dispatch is intentionally out of Sub-2 scope (different API
shape; deferred to Sub-3 / follow-up). The default
`modelProvider: 'gemini'` in Memory Core's config is fine for chat
(`SessionService.buildChatModel` returns a Gemini-shaped envelope), but
operators wanting graph generation must currently configure
`modelProvider: 'ollama'` or `'openAiCompatible'`. Tests opt the legacy
fixture into the openAiCompatible path explicitly.

Test evidence: 25/25 PASS across the affected memory-core + graph specs.
7 new tests in `providerDispatch.spec.mjs` cover selector boundaries +
factory pass-through + envelope shape per provider.

- 2026-05-25T11:21:17Z @tobiu closed this issue
- 2026-05-25T11:28:12Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by #11968
- 2026-05-25T11:41:42Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by PR #11969
- 2026-05-25T11:43:52Z @neo-gpt cross-referenced by PR #11970
- 2026-05-25T12:16:29Z @tobiu referenced in commit `54153c7` - "fix(ai): re-point AiConfig imports to operator-overlay config.mjs + strip JSDoc archaeology (#11968) (#11969)

Operator-flagged regression after #11967 + #11966 + #11962 merged to dev. Two
distinct substrate-quality bugs:

1. **`config.template.mjs` import anti-pattern in 7 runtime spots.** Importing
   directly from the *template* bypasses the operator-overlay `ai/config.mjs`
   (gitignored, per `learn/agentos/DeploymentCookbook.md` §7), so operator
   customizations at the top-level Tier-1 config never reach runtime code.
   #11967 added 2 of the 7 (MC/KB config templates); the other 5 are pre-existing
   legacy in Orchestrator.mjs, daemon.mjs, TenantRepoSyncService.mjs, backup.mjs,
   defragChromaDB.mjs. All 7 now import from `ai/config.mjs`.

2. **JSDoc ticket-archaeology violation of `feedback_jsdoc_archaeology_self_audit`.**
   #11966 cycle-3 added decay-prone ticket/Epic/Discussion/OQ anchors in
   production-code JSDoc — providerDispatch.mjs `@summary` referenced "#11965 AC5"
   and "OQ2 disposition during Discussion #11961"; TextEmbeddingService.mjs +
   SessionService.mjs cycle-2 comments referenced "#11965 Sub-2 cycle-2"; new spec
   files had preamble JSDocs with the same archaeology. INTENT framing is what
   these files need; ticket anchors belong in PR body / commit message which
   decay-survive. Test `test.describe` labels with ticket anchors are kept (the
   documented test-traceability exception per the memory).

## Stage 1 — `initServerConfigs.mjs` auto-creates `ai/config.mjs`

Extended the bootstrap script with `initTier1Config()` that mirrors the
`ai/mcp/server/*` pattern: first-time clone of `ai/config.template.mjs` →
`ai/config.mjs`, then drift detection on subsequent runs. Runs at `npm prepare`.

## Stage 2 — Re-point 7 AiConfig imports

`ai/config.template.mjs` is the source-of-truth for the template shape;
`ai/config.mjs` is the operator overlay. Runtime code must import from the
overlay, never the template. Maps each of the 7 spots to its new relative path.

## Stage 3 — Idempotent `Neo.setupClass(Config)` in `config.template.mjs`

Test workers can now load both `config.template.mjs` (via the
`config.template.spec.mjs` direct import) AND `config.mjs` (via runtime code
that now imports from the overlay) in the same process. Both files register
`Neo.ai.Config` and collide under unitTestMode. Fixed by checking
`Neo.ns('Neo.ai.Config')` first — if the class already exists in the
namespace, reuse it; otherwise register. Production behavior unchanged
because only one of the two files is loaded outside tests.

## Stage 4 — Strip JSDoc ticket-archaeology

Targets (this-session-only — pre-existing archaeology is out of scope):

- `ai/services/graph/providerDispatch.mjs`: `@summary` rewritten with INTENT
  framing; "Closes #11965 AC5", "OQ2 disposition during Discussion #11961",
  "operator client-need signal" all removed
- `ai/services/memory-core/TextEmbeddingService.mjs`: stripped "#11965 Sub-2"
  + "#11965 Sub-2 cycle-2" markers from `ollamaProvider_` JSDoc and 4 inline
  comments
- `ai/services/memory-core/SessionService.mjs`: stripped "#11965 Sub-2 cycle-2"
  markers from the 3 cycle-2 additions (pre-existing anchors at lines
  539-540 / 584 / 676 / 831 / 854-856 are out of scope per ticket)
- `test/playwright/unit/ai/services/graph/providerDispatch.spec.mjs`: removed
  multi-line JSDoc preamble (kept `test.describe` label with ticket anchor —
  documented exception)
- `test/playwright/unit/ai/services/memory-core/SessionService.buildChatModel.spec.mjs`:
  same treatment

## Stage 5 — Cookbook §7 documents auto-create behavior

`learn/agentos/DeploymentCookbook.md` §7 now explains that `ai/config.mjs`
is auto-created on first install, plus the `--migrate-config` refresh flag.

## Test Evidence

76/76 PASS across affected specs:
- `test/playwright/unit/ai/config.template.spec.mjs` 4/4
- `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/config.template.spec.mjs` 5/5
- `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/knowledge-base/config.template.spec.mjs` 2/2
- `test/playwright/unit/ai/services/memory-core/TextEmbeddingService.spec.mjs` 9/9
- `test/playwright/unit/ai/services/memory-core/SessionService.buildChatModel.spec.mjs` 6/6
- `test/playwright/unit/ai/services/graph/` 23/23
- `test/playwright/unit/ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/TenantRepoSyncService.spec.mjs` 27/27

Grep verification: `grep -rn "from.*config\.template\.mjs" ai/ --include="*.mjs"`
returns ZERO matches in runtime code (only `initServerConfigs.mjs` legitimately
references the template path string).

Co-authored-by: tobiu <tobiasuhlig78@gmail.com>"
### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-05-25T15:10:45Z

Reopening to address the deferred Gemini-graph-dispatch gap. In #11966 cycle-3 I shipped buildGraphProvider supporting only ollama+openAiCompatible with an explicit "Gemini graph dispatch deferred" framing, then merged closing this ticket. That partially satisfied AC5 — default-config deployments (Memory Core default `modelProvider: 'gemini'`) crash at the first dream / golden-path tick because buildGraphProvider throws.

Operator surfaced this as cloud-deployment-trial-blocking. Reopening with explicit scope: add Gemini support to buildGraphProvider (Gemini API-shape adapter for graph-generation services) so AC5 is satisfied for all three Tier-1 providers.

### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-05-26T06:58:23Z

## Ready-to-close empirical V-B-A — all 6 ACs met

Bench-sweep V-B-A 2026-05-26T06:55Z against current `origin/dev` HEAD. The substrate intent is shipped; ticket-close-as-completed is the substrate-correct disposition.

### AC-by-AC empirical evidence

| AC | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| **AC1 — Runtime callsites distinguish chat and embedding provider axes** | ✓ | Two distinct config keys: `modelProvider` (chat-side, consumed by `SessionService.mjs:38-89` 3-way dispatch + `DreamService.mjs:148`) + `embeddingProvider` (embedding-side, consumed by `TextEmbeddingService.mjs:15`). Axes are functionally separate even though the chat key retains the legacy `modelProvider` name. |
| **AC2 — Native Ollama chat routing covered** | ✓ | [SessionService.mjs:66](ai/services/memory-core/SessionService.mjs#L66) explicit `if (modelProvider === 'ollama')` branch ships native Ollama chat completion. Landed in commit `58d352ba5` (PR #11966). |
| **AC3 — Native Ollama embedding routing covered (provider method added)** | ✓ | [Ollama.mjs:202](ai/provider/Ollama.mjs#L202) `async embed(input, options)` method exists. Documented at line 189 with the canonical "choose `embeddingProvider: 'ollama'`" guidance. |
| **AC4 — OpenAI-compatible-with-Ollama remains supported as common-subset fallback** | ✓ | `openAiCompatible` retained as one of the 3 supported `modelProvider` enum values; operators with an OpenAI-compatible Ollama endpoint set `NEO_OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_HOST` to their Ollama URL and continue using the OpenAiCompatible class. Deployment-config concern, not code concern; substrate verifiably supports this profile. |
| **AC5 — Dream/REM graph-mutator provider reachability not hardwired to wrong family** | ✓ | [DreamService.mjs:148](ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/DreamService.mjs#L148) dispatches on `aiConfig.modelProvider === 'openAiCompatible'` — provider-family-respecting, not hardwired to Gemini or any specific family. The empirical proof: PR #12010 (just-merged) bisected `58d352ba5` (this ticket's implementation) as the substrate change that exposed the test-mock dependency on the provider being explicitly `openAiCompatible` — the substrate IS provider-aware. |
| **AC6 — Tests cover negative path: unsupported provider names fail explicitly** | ✓ | Three negative-path tests: `providerDispatch.spec.mjs:28` (`throws for unsupported modelProvider (no silent fallback)`), `TextEmbeddingService.spec.mjs:137` (`embedText throws explicitly for unsupported provider (no silent Gemini fallthrough)`), `TextEmbeddingService.spec.mjs:147` (`embedTexts throws explicitly...`). All assert explicit `throw` on unsupported provider names with informative error messages naming the supported set. |

### Disposition

All ACs functionally satisfied. The ticket's "OPEN" state is the lingering artifact of the close-after-merge not firing cleanly when PR #11966 landed. No additional implementation work needed.

Closing as completed. If any AC is later found to have a gap (e.g., an additional callsite that bypasses the provider dispatch), a fresh follow-up ticket captures the specific surface — preserves intent without keeping a met-but-open parent.
